title: lobelia flexuosa subsp. intermedia
intro: Lobelia flexuosa subsp. intermedia is a subspecies of the Lobelia flexuosa plant, also known as the twisted lobelia or blue cardinal flower, which belongs to the family Campanulaceae. It is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, particularly in the Mediterranean area, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and also ease muscle spasms and joint pain. Herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, can be made from the leaves and flowers of the plant, and are often used to promote overall well-being and health, particularly in the treatment of respiratory and musculoskeletal conditions.
